hero_summary: In modern Arabic usage, Shahnaz is often understood as a branch of the Hijaz family, but the more precise analytical reading presents it as Hijazkar transposed to Dukah (D), not merely simple Hijaz on D.
short_identity: A practical Hijaz-family branch, but analytically closer to Hijazkar on Dukah (D).

quick_facts.upper_jins: A full Hijazkar-type upper construction, not just simple Hijaz
quick_facts.common_start: It often begins by stabilizing Dukah (D), then clearly bringing out the Hijazkar color
quick_facts.character: Hijaz in family belonging, Hijazkar in essence, and broader in structure than basic Hijaz

sayr.summary: Shahnaz should not be reduced to a mere ascent through a Hijaz jins and then a descent. In this formulation it is understood as Hijazkar transposed to Dukah (D): it begins by stabilizing Dukah as tonic, then clearly shows a Hijazkar color rather than simple Hijaz alone. After that, it expands into the upper register while preserving the maqam’s identity, then returns and cadences on Dukah (D). The decisive element here is therefore not merely the tonic, but the upper structure that makes it Shahnaz rather than ordinary Hijaz.
resting_tones: Dukah (D): the tonic and the specific transposed resting degree of Shahnaz
Nawa (G): still a very important tone inside the broader Hijaz-based structure
Kardan (upper C) and the surrounding tones: important in upper expansion because Shahnaz works strongly in the higher register as well
motion_notes: Shahnaz is not simply Hijaz on D, but Hijazkar on D
The difference between it and ordinary Hijaz appears in the breadth of structure, not in the tonic alone
It should not be played as though it were only simple Hijaz, because that strips away its upper identity
The strongest analytical source here confirms its identity as a transposition of Hijazkar more than as an isolated scale-based maqam
common_path: Expand into the upper register while preserving identity

common_modulations: It is understood inside the Hijaz family in practical Arabic classification
But analytically it is closer to Hijazkar transposed to Dukah (D)
If it is treated as only simple Hijaz, its Hijazkar-specific identity is lost
The available documentation here is stronger in confirming the theoretical identity than in supplying a long list of extensively analyzed Arabic repertoire examples
